key_insight:
C65 TWO DELIVERABLES:

(1) CIAP META-THEOREM (SI #45 candidate):
CIA_i Independence Principle — when CIA_i is p-independent, ceiling(p)=(1-p)*Gamma algebraically.

creiling_TCA(r)=(1-r)*Gamma and ceiling_GCRSC(CR)=(1-CR)*Gamma_domain are not analogous theorems. They are Corollary A and B of the same CIAP meta-theorem. The linearity is not domain-specific — it is the algebraic consequence of CIA_i p-independence.

When CIAP fails (adaptive adversary: CIA_i decreasing in p, adversary ramps up step size when protection is weaker): ceiling_adaptive(p) >= (1-p)*Gamma (super-linear). ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC become LOWER BOUNDS in adaptive adversary domains.

Domain CIAP status:
- RLHF/TCA: CIAP holds (gradient signal not p-responsive)
- GCRSC/Goodhart fixed-strategy: CIAP holds
- LLM-assisted gaming: CIAP may fail (adaptive optimizer responds to CR)
- Competitive adversarial (red-teaming, markets): CIAP likely fails

Confidence implications:
- SI #34: 0.86-0.87 -> 0.87-0.88 (structural unification elevates)
- SI #36: 0.79-0.81 -> 0.81-0.83 (GCRSC now a corollary of confirmed principle)
- SI #45: 0.84 (tight algebraic proof; uncertainty in CIAP domain applicability)

(2) SI #44 AAA DC#3 — CLINICAL/MEDICAL JUDGMENT:
CA=patient outcome; OA=clinical presentation/peer approval/professional identity.
Mechanism: expert-authority gradient — role-mediated epistemic failure.
Evidence: Croskerry 2002, Friedman 2005, Leape 1994, Dawes 2002, Groopman 2007.
Distinct from DC#1 (individual approval) and DC#2 (group cohesion).
SI #44 three mechanisms confirmed: individual approval pressure / collective cohesion / expert authority gradient.
SI #44 confidence: 0.77-0.79 -> 0.79-0.81.

PASSIVE: AIIP ZR cycle 13 (C55-C65). AATE ZR cycle 10 (C56-C65). D4 streak 57.

resolved_questions: Are ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC the same theorem? YES — both are Corollary A and B of CIAP Meta-Theorem. Algebraically identical. (C65)
When do linear ceiling theorems hold exactly vs. as lower bounds? CIAP-holding domains: exact. CIAP-failing (adaptive adversary): lower bound only. (C65)
SI #44 DC#3: Is clinical/medical judgment a valid third mechanism? YES — expert-authority gradient mechanism distinct from DC#1, DC#2, with strong independent evidence. (C65)
